records.ety: [F. divers, L. diversus turned in different directions, different, p. p. of divertere. See Divert, and cf. Diverse.]

defs: Different in kind or species; diverse.
quotes: Every sect of them hath a divers posture.
Thou shalt not sow thy vineyard with divers seeds.

defs: Several; sundry; various; more than one, but not a great number; as, divers philosophers. Also used substantively or pronominally.
notes: ☞ Divers is now limited to the plural; as, divers ways (not divers way). Besides plurality it ordinarily implies variety of kind.
